role: A dropdown chip control that lets the user override which embedded item-store (space) the active projection lens ranges over, populated dynamically from the backend layer registry.
capabilities: Fetch and sort the list of embedded spaces from /api/layers
Conditionally render only for bindings that self-report a ranged-over space and are not by_nucleation
Display the currently effective space (explicit override or binding default)
Toggle an animated dropdown menu of available spaces
Select a space override and propagate it via setSpace callback
Close the menu via scrim click or selection
Apply UI address stamps for telemetry
substance_why: The file implements complete data fetching, visibility logic, rendering, animation, selection handling, and telemetry stamping for the space picker.
interactions_present: Click chip button to open/close the space menu
Click a menu item to select a space and close the menu
Click the scrim to close the menu
interactions_missing: No keyboard navigation, Escape-to-close, or focus trapping visible
No loading, error, or empty-state feedback for the layers fetch
No search/filter for potentially long space lists
performance_risks: fetchLayers is called on every mount with no caching, deduplication, or suspense integration
The full spaces list is rendered unconditionally, which may become costly if many spaces are registered
Framer-motion AnimatePresence runs on every open/close
reuse_why: The registry-true dynamic space-selection concept is worth keeping, but the component should be rebuilt on top of a generic dropdown/menu primitive and a separate data hook.
decomposition_hints: Extract a reusable useSpaces/useLayers data hook from the fetch/useEffect logic
Split the generic chip dropdown shell (button, menu, scrim, animation) into a shared primitive
Move binding-driven visibility rules (by_nucleation, binding.space) into a projection selector or hook
Separate stamp/telemetry concerns from the component markup
